pred_answer: The short title of the Customs Act is the Customs Act 1960.
The purpose of customs duty under the Customs Act is to raise revenue for the Government.

The Director-General of Customs has and may exercise and perform all the powers, authorities, duties and functions conferred and imposed on the Comptroller of Customs by the Customs Act 1960, subject to such limitations as the Minister may think fit to impose.
The full name of the CPF law in Singapore is the Pension Fund Act 1995.
The main objective of the Central Provident Fund scheme is to provide a retirement and pension scheme for employees.
Generally, all employees are required to make CPF contributions, except for those who are exempted under the Employment of Foreign Manpower Act 1990. (Section 3, Central Provident Fund Act 1953)
CareShield Life is designed to provide long-term care financial support to severely disabled persons.

Payouts typically begin from the day the person with severe disability is admitted to a long-term care institution or receives long-term care at home. (CareShield Life and Long-Term Care Act 2019, s 4(2))

A person who contravenes any provision of the Customs Act 1960 or any regulation made under section 3 of the Customs Act 1960 shall be guilty of an offence and shall be liable on conviction to a fine not exceeding $100,000 or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 12 months or to both.
The Customs Act 1960, CPF Act 1953, and CareShield Life Act 2019 are all economic legislation.
